title: Cheap Boat Rental in Marbella: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Cheap boat rental in Marbella starts with shared sailing trips from around EUR 35 per person, while a small self-drive motorboat from Puerto Banus or Marbella's own Puerto Deportivo runs from roughly EUR 250 a day. BoatHire24 lists budget options alongside crewed yachts so you can match a boat to your wallet.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella sits on the Costa del Sol, and most affordable trips leave from a handful of well-known harbours near the old town. Knowing the departure points helps you compare prices, since a quieter marina often costs less than a glamorous one.

  • Puerto Banus: the famous marina just west of Marbella, busy with both budget shared trips and premium yachts.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the town's central sport harbour, handy for self-drive motorboats.
  • Puerto Cabopino: a smaller marina east of Marbella near the Artola dunes, often cheaper for short hires.
  • La Bajadilla: Marbella's working fishing port, a low-key spot for local boat trips.
  • Estepona: a neighbouring port roughly 17 nautical miles west, worth checking for keener rates.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Cheap boat rental in Marbella ranges from about EUR 35 per person for a shared tour to EUR 250 for a small motorboat half-day. Prices climb with size, season and crew, but there are honest entry points at every level, and you can book a charter online once you have compared a few harbours and dates.

  • Shared dolphin or coastal tour: from around EUR 35 per person for a couple of hours.
  • Small self-drive motorboat, half-day: from around EUR 250, often with no licence needed for low-power craft.
  • Catamaran, full day with skipper: from roughly EUR 900 to EUR 1,600 depending on size and group.
  • Crewed motor yacht or sailing yacht: from around EUR 1,800 per day, fuel and crew extra.

Which boat to rent in Marbella

The cheapest sensible choice depends on your group size and whether you want to drive yourself. These are the common categories, from budget self-drive boats up to a motor yacht for a special day.

  1. Small motorboat: best for couples or four friends wanting a self-drive half-day, seats up to 6, from EUR 250.
  2. RIB speedboat: best for quick coastal hops and snorkelling stops, seats up to 8, from EUR 350.
  3. Sailing yacht: best for a slow, low-cost full day under canvas, seats up to 8, from EUR 650.
  4. Catamaran: best for stable family days and groups, seats up to 12, from EUR 900.
  5. Motor yacht: best for a premium celebration, and this motor yacht, the Princess F70, seats up to 12, from EUR 2,500.

Do you need a licence to rent a cheap boat in Marbella?

In Spain you can hire low-power boats without a licence, while larger or faster craft need a recognised qualification, so the operator confirms exact requirements. Many budget motorboats are rated below the licence threshold, so first-timers favour them. High season runs from June to September, when the Levante easterly wind can build through the afternoon.

Book morning slots in July and August: the sea off Marbella is usually calmest before midday, and the afternoon Levante wind can make budget self-drive boats less comfortable for nervous passengers.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 35 per person shared, EUR 250 self-drive half-day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: two hours up to a full eight-hour day.
  • Capacity: typically 6 to 12 guests on the cheaper boats.
  • Currency: euro (EUR).
  • Year referenced: 2026.

How does booking a cheap boat actually work?

You pick a boat and date, send a request to the owner, and confirm once availability and the deposit are agreed. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. Reading each listing's fuel and deposit terms is the simplest way to avoid surprises on the day.

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

What is the cheapest way to get on the water in Marbella?

A shared coastal or dolphin-watching trip is usually the cheapest option, from around EUR 35 per person for roughly two hours, since you split the boat and skipper with other passengers rather than hiring the whole vessel.

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

You can hire certain low-power motorboats in Marbella without a licence under Spanish rules, which suits beginners, though the operator confirms the exact power limit and any safety briefing before you set off.

When is boat rental cheapest in Marbella?

Rates are generally lowest outside the June to September peak, so spring and autumn often bring better value, calmer crowds and pleasant water temperatures while many boats remain available along the coast.

How many people fit on a budget boat?

Most affordable self-drive motorboats carry up to six guests, while small catamarans and shared tours often seat ten to twelve, so match the boat capacity to your group before you compare prices.

Marbella rewards planning: book early in the day and compare a couple of harbours to enjoy the Costa del Sol without overspending. To weigh budget motorboats against catamarans and yachts, browse the listings and confirm terms with each owner.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
